purpose: Deliver the advanced Eclipse control surface in two phases: finished contract foundation and pending runnable Eclipse plugin runtime implementation with test-heavy hardening.
depends_on: Eclipse foundation contracts/tests/docs (completed, runtime still blocked)
goal: Ship a real, buildable, test-covered Eclipse runtime plugin surface for Ananta, while preserving hub ownership, policy enforcement, bounded context, explicit degraded states, and auditability.
success_criteria: Eclipse plugin runtime project exists with plugin metadata and deterministic build.
Core Eclipse commands execute against backend APIs with bounded user-visible context.
Task/artifact/approval/audit/repair surfaces are operational and governance-safe.
TUI launch integration is explicit, safe and observable.
Unit, integration, security, contract and smoke checks enforce runtime behavior and guardrails.
Docs and merge-readiness evidence match the real implementation state.
architecture_guardrails: Eclipse remains a thin client surface, never a second control plane.
No orchestration, approval policy or governance logic is duplicated client-side.
All risky actions are backend-gated, permission-aware and auditable.
No implicit repair execution, no implicit file mutation, no hidden command execution.
Degraded states are explicit and actionable, never masked as success.
Browser fallback remains available for deep/high-risk admin workflows.

milestones.title: Foundation analysis and developer work modes
Foundation approval, audit and repair deep views
Foundation TUI launch and integration strategy
Foundation tests, docs and baseline report
Runtime hardening, tests and release readiness

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse scope boundary define (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Advanced feature boundary is documented.
Thin-adapter and control-plane separation is explicit.
High-risk views are marked backend-gated or browser-first.

tasks.title: Eclipse advanced view model inventory create (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: View inventory maps capabilities and API dependencies.
Read-only vs read-write classification exists.
Missing endpoints are explicit instead of silently mocked.

tasks.title: Permission and capability model define (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Capability and permission semantics are modeled.
Denied actions are represented explicitly.
No client-side permission escalation path exists.

tasks.title: Advanced navigation and perspective layout define (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Recommended Eclipse perspective/layout is defined.
Context-preserving navigation model exists.
Browser fallback links are modeled.

tasks.title: Analysis mode registry implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Multiple analysis modes are modeled and testable.
Modes map to official Ananta flows.
Capability-aware enable/disable behavior is covered.

tasks.title: Analysis launcher view implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Launcher contract includes explicit context preview.
Submission maps to backend goal/task semantics.
Result linkage model is defined.

tasks.title: Multi-result analysis history view implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: History model covers mode, status and links.
Reopen and stale-object behavior are represented.
Graceful degradation is defined.

tasks.title: Review and proposal workbench implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Review/proposal model includes diff rendering semantics.
No auto-apply behavior is enforced.
Approval-aware flow contracts exist.

tasks.title: Blueprint and profile selection view implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Blueprint/work-profile view model exists.
Selection is explicit metadata in submission model.
Unavailable profile behavior is defined.

tasks.title: Ananta system overview view implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: System overview model covers health/capabilities/runtime state.
Read-only default behavior is explicit.
Failure/degraded state semantics are represented.

tasks.title: Worker and queue visibility view implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Worker/queue visibility contract exists.
Refresh behavior and backend load guard are modeled.
No unsafe control action is exposed without capability.

tasks.title: Provider and model runtime view implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Provider/model runtime read model exists.
Sensitive config values are redacted.
Permission-aware access model is defined.

tasks.title: Task artifact trace explorer implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Goal-task-artifact-trace relationship model exists.
Trace and audit reference visibility is modeled.
Deep-link fallback contract is defined.

tasks.title: Runtime diagnostics and degraded-state panel implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Degraded state taxonomy is modeled.
Actionable operator hints are represented.
False-success masking is explicitly forbidden.

tasks.title: Configuration surface strategy define (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Config scope split (read-only/edit/browser-first) is documented.
Risky config writes are backend-gated and auditable by rule.
No raw unsafe editor path is allowed.

tasks.title: Read-only configuration view implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Read-only config view model exists.
Secrets/tokens are redacted by contract.
Unavailable categories are explicit.

tasks.title: Safe configuration edit flow implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Safe edit flow model includes preview/confirm semantics.
Backend validation/audit dependency is explicit.
Failed writes must not mutate assumed client state.

tasks.title: Control actions registry implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Action registry contains risk/capability classification.
Unknown actions fail closed by model.
Dangerous actions require explicit confirmation.

tasks.title: Approval queue advanced view implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Approval queue model includes enough review context.
Approve/reject is backend API-only by rule.
Stale/denied/already-handled state handling exists.

tasks.title: Audit explorer view implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Audit explorer contract covers summaries and traces.
Sensitive values are redacted in representation.
Filtering semantics are modeled.

tasks.title: Repair session explorer implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Repair session view model exists.
No implicit execution behavior is enforced.
Risk and approval status visibility is modeled.

tasks.title: Repair review and approval flow implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Repair approval flow model includes explicit confirmation.
Dry-run-first semantics are represented.
Trace/audit result linkage is modeled.

tasks.title: Policy denial and blocked action view implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Policy denial view model exists.
Navigation from denial to related context is represented.
No governance-bypass workaround is modeled.

tasks.title: TUI integration strategy decide (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Integration option comparison is documented.
Security implications of process launch are evaluated.
Safe default path is selected.

tasks.title: Launch external Ananta TUI action implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: External TUI launch contract exists.
Launch is explicit and confirmable.
Profile context transfer and secret handling are modeled.

tasks.title: Eclipse terminal integration evaluate (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Terminal-hosting evaluation exists.
Implemented-vs-deferred criteria are defined.
Terminal lifecycle error visibility is modeled.

tasks.title: TUI bridge status view implement (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: TUI bridge status model exists.
Missing runtime behavior is explicit.
No fake embedded TUI representation exists.

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse unit tests add (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Unit-level contract builders are covered by tests.
Degraded and capability-missing behavior is tested.
Tests run without live backend dependency.

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse integration tests add (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Contract integration semantics are covered in tests.
Malformed and denied behavior paths are exercised.
Policy-bypass negative expectations are tested.

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse manual smoke checklist add (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Manual smoke checklist exists and is scoped.
Expected success/failure signals are documented.
Required vs optional checks are separated.

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse documentation update (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Foundation docs describe modeled view set and boundaries.
Browser-first and governance limits are explicit.
No runnable Eclipse runtime claim is made.

tasks.title: Advanced Eclipse merge readiness report generate (foundation)
acceptance_criteria: Foundation readiness report exists.
Implemented-vs-deferred contracts are listed.
Safety and branch-merge caveats are explicit.

tasks.title: Runtime unblocking decision and scope freeze
acceptance_criteria: Decision explicitly moves Eclipse from blocked to runtime-delivery scope for this track.
Runtime MVP scope and out-of-scope items are frozen.
Decision is reflected in runtime status and docs before implementation starts.

tasks.title: Create Eclipse plugin runtime project layout
acceptance_criteria: Repository contains a real Eclipse plugin project directory.
Project package namespace and plugin id are stable and documented.
Layout supports deterministic local and CI build.

tasks.title: Add plugin metadata and manifest artifacts
acceptance_criteria: plugin.xml and META-INF/MANIFEST.MF are present and valid.
build.properties (or equivalent) exists for reproducible packaging.
Command/view extension points are registered without runtime load errors.

tasks.title: Implement reproducible plugin build pipeline
acceptance_criteria: Tycho or Gradle build path is selected and implemented.
Plugin build succeeds from clean checkout with documented command.
Build path is CI-friendly and non-interactive.

tasks.title: Implement Java backend client core
acceptance_criteria: Client supports health, capabilities, goals, tasks, artifacts, approvals, audit and repair endpoints.
Typed response mapping includes degraded-state semantics.
No silent fallback swallows backend failures.

tasks.title: Implement secure profile and token handling
acceptance_criteria: Profile persistence excludes raw tokens/secrets.
Log/error channels redact sensitive values consistently.
Profile/auth validation errors are explicit and actionable.

tasks.title: Implement runtime capability and permission handshake
acceptance_criteria: Runtime handshake gates commands and actions by capability/permission.
Denied actions render explicit policy-denied state.
No action appears enabled when backend denies it.

tasks.title: Register Eclipse runtime commands and handlers
acceptance_criteria: Analyze/review/patch/new/evolve commands are registered and callable.
Handlers are wired through client layer and capability gates.
Missing capabilities degrade explicitly instead of failing silently.

tasks.title: Implement bounded editor/workspace context capture runtime
acceptance_criteria: Runtime captures current file, selection and bounded project context.
User-visible context preview is shown before send.
Unrelated files are not implicitly included.

tasks.title: Implement goal submission runtime panel
acceptance_criteria: Goal panel submits through backend goal/task flow.
Operation preset and profile metadata are attached explicitly.
Result links to task/artifact views are available.

tasks.title: Implement analyze/review/patch runtime execution flows
acceptance_criteria: Analyze/review/patch commands execute end-to-end against backend APIs.
Success/failure/degraded output is rendered explicitly.
No local file mutation occurs without backend-approved flow.

tasks.title: Implement new/evolve project runtime flows
acceptance_criteria: New/evolve commands submit official project goal flows.
Blueprint/work-profile selection is passed explicitly.
Runtime errors and policy denials are visible and traceable.

tasks.title: Implement runtime task list and task detail views
acceptance_criteria: Task list/detail views load live backend data with refresh controls.
Status, review-required and next-step markers are visible.
Stale/missing task states degrade safely.

tasks.title: Implement runtime artifact view and diff rendering
acceptance_criteria: Artifact list/detail renders live data and links to tasks.
Diff/review artifacts render with file references.
Unsupported artifact types degrade to text/browser fallback.

tasks.title: Implement runtime approval queue actions
acceptance_criteria: Approval queue loads pending items with review context.
Approve/reject requires explicit user confirmation.
Actions call backend approval APIs only and return audit/trace references.

tasks.title: Implement runtime audit explorer
acceptance_criteria: Audit explorer loads summaries and detailed entries from backend.
Filters (severity/type/time/object) work in runtime view.
Sensitive fields are redacted consistently.

tasks.title: Implement runtime repair explorer and guarded step actions
acceptance_criteria: Repair explorer shows sessions, diagnosis, steps, verification and outcome.
Runtime defaults to read-only unless backend capability allows step approvals.
No repair execution occurs on open/refresh.

tasks.title: Implement runtime TUI launch and status panel
acceptance_criteria: TUI launch action starts documented command explicitly.
Profile/endpoint context handoff is supported safely.
Launch failures and missing TUI runtime are explicit.

tasks.title: Implement runtime policy-denied and browser-fallback UX
acceptance_criteria: Policy-denied paths render structured denial info with next steps.
Deep links to browser fallback are available for complex/risky workflows.
Fallback behavior never bypasses governance.

tasks.title: Add runtime Java unit tests
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover command dispatch, client mapping and state models.
Tests cover degraded states, capability gating and profile validation.
Tests run deterministically without live backend.

tasks.title: Add runtime Eclipse integration/UI tests
acceptance_criteria: Integration tests cover command execution and main views.
UI tests cover context preview and explicit confirmation paths.
Tests verify no implicit apply/execute behavior exists.

tasks.title: Add runtime security and governance tests
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify no policy bypass via Eclipse actions.
Tests verify token/secret redaction across logs and errors.
Tests cover denied, unauthorized, stale and malformed backend responses.

tasks.title: Add runtime API contract compatibility tests
acceptance_criteria: Contract tests lock request/response fields used by runtime plugin.
Breaking backend shape changes fail tests clearly.
Degraded-state payload shapes are included.

tasks.title: Add runtime headless smoke checks and CI lane
acceptance_criteria: Headless smoke validates plugin load, command registration and one mocked golden path.
CI lane runs runtime unit/integration/smoke checks.
Runtime claims are blocked if smoke evidence is missing.

tasks.title: Run runtime documentation truth pass
acceptance_criteria: Docs only claim runnable Eclipse runtime features that actually exist.
Deferred/blocked and browser-first areas remain explicit.
Install/build/run/troubleshoot steps are validated against real runtime.

tasks.title: Generate runtime merge readiness report
acceptance_criteria: Report lists runtime-implemented views/actions and remaining blockers.
Report references unit/integration/security/contract/smoke evidence.
Report states merge-safe vs feature-flag-only recommendation explicitly.

execution_order_summary: Stage 1: Foundation contracts, docs and baseline tests are complete (EAC-T01..EAC-T32).
Stage 2: Unblock Eclipse runtime and create real plugin project/build skeleton (EAC-T33..EAC-T39).
Stage 3: Implement runtime commands, context packaging, views and operations (EAC-T40..EAC-T51).
Stage 4: Add test-heavy hardening, CI/smoke gates, docs truth and merge readiness report (EAC-T52..EAC-T58).

alignment_issues_found: Eclipse runtime command/view operational block is now implemented (EAC-T40..EAC-T51) with registry, handlers, bounded context capture and runtime view wiring.
Runtime hardening/release block (EAC-T52..EAC-T58) is implemented with Java unit/integration/security/contract suites, headless smoke gate, CI lane and merge-readiness report.

summary_notes: Correction applied: prior EAC todo marked everything as todo, but real repository evidence already contains substantial foundation implementation in service contracts, tests and docs.
First runtime block implemented: EAC-T33..EAC-T39 (runtime unblocking, plugin skeleton/metadata, build bootstrap, Java client core, token security, capability gate).
Second runtime block implemented: EAC-T40..EAC-T51 (command registry/handlers, bounded context capture, goal panel runtime, task/artifact/approval/audit/repair views, TUI bridge, policy fallback UX).
Runtime hardening block implemented: EAC-T52..EAC-T58 (Java unit/integration/security/contract tests, headless smoke gate, CI lane, docs truth pass, merge readiness report).
This plan preserves SRP/DIP boundaries by separating foundation modeling from runtime plugin implementation and by keeping governance decisions in backend APIs.
Runtime track is now complete at 58/58 done with merge-safe recommendation captured in runtime readiness data.
